title: IRS deadline looms: Millions of US taxpayers could claim COVID-era penalty refunds by July 10
content:
• Eligible US taxpayers must submit COVID-era penalty refund or protective claims by July 10, 2026.
• The federal court ruling that opened the door for these refunds came in the case of Kwong vs the United States.
• The national COVID-19 emergency officially ended on May 11, 2023, potentially shifting tax filing deadlines for tax years 2019 through 2022 to July 10, 2023.

title: Iran begins week-long Ali Khamenei's funeral, but at what cost? Companies 'forced to pay' for food, soup, tea — report
content:
• The funeral ceremonies for late Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei began on July 3, with his burial scheduled for July 9.
• Over 1,000 billion rials (more than $570,000) was reportedly taken from automobile manufacturers to fund the funeral.
• The average monthly income in Iran is around $150, which is significantly below the estimated poverty line of $350 for a family.

title: Govt directs Apple, Google to remove 7 apps allegedly misused to disable e-rickshaw batteries, says report—Latest update
content:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) directed Google and Apple to remove seven apps, including BAT-BMS, SMART BMS, and LOSSIGY, from their stores for allegedly disabling e-rickshaw batteries.
• Disabling e-rickshaw batteries is a criminal offense under sections 66 and 43 of the Information Technology Act 2000, punishable with up to 3 years imprisonment and fines of ₹5 lakh.
• S Krishnan, Secretary of MeitY, stated that app stores have a responsibility to ensure applications do not pose risks to public safety or facilitate unlawful activities.

title: Brutal US heat dome threatens July 4th, World Cup weekend; 100°F in New York City
content:
• Over 180 million people spanning Kansas to Maine were under extreme heat warnings or advisories on Friday, according to the US Weather Prediction Center.
• PJM Interconnection LLC, the largest grid serving nearly a fifth of Americans from Washington to Chicago, operated in an emergency (lowest of three levels) for the third consecutive day, ordering all power plants to be available at maximum levels.
• Consolidated Edison Co., New York City's electric utility, asked about 133,000 customers in Northern Brooklyn to conserve energy, throttling voltage back by 8% for equipment repairs.

title: Meta in more trouble with govt? IT Minister summons company over Instagram ads promoting child sexual abuse: Report
content:
• On 1 July, the Indian government issued a notice to WhatsApp, directing it to pause its proposed username feature and giving Meta three days to explain concerns over increased online fraud and impersonation, citing the IT Act.
• IT Secretary S Krishnan confirmed that the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) is exploring a dedicated regulatory framework or separate legislation for Artificial Intelligence (AI), indicating a potential new legal approach beyond current IT rules.
• The government reminded Meta that WhatsApp, as a significant social media intermediary, must comply with due diligence obligations under the IT Act and rules, particularly concerning features like the proposed usernames.

title: Ali Khamenei Funeral LIVE Updates: Donald Trump takes dig at Iran, late Supreme Leader's casket unveiled
content:
• Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, the 86-year-old Supreme Leader of Iran, was killed in US-Israel strikes on Tehran on February 28.
• His burial is scheduled for July 9 at the Imam Reza shrine in Mashhad, following a delay of over four months due to wartime conditions.
• Prominent foreign dignitaries like Pakistan's PM Shehbaz Sharif, Russia's Security Council Deputy Chairman Dmitry Medvedev, and India's Governor of Bihar Syed Ata Hasnain attended the funeral ceremonies.

title: Operationalising GST refunds for foreign tourists to promote retail exports: FIEO
content:
• Section 15 of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax (IGST) Act, 2017, provides for a GST refund mechanism for foreign tourists, which remains unimplemented despite nearly nine years since GST's introduction.
• Export credit has contracted by nearly 14% in the recent period, leading the Federation of Indian Export Organisations (FIEO) to request the government to extend priority sector export credit.
• India aims to achieve an ambitious target of becoming a USD 2 trillion export economy, for which a responsive export credit ecosystem is deemed indispensable.

title: India's forex reserves fall $5.65 billion to $666.93 billion in week ended June 26
content:
• India's forex reserves fell by $5.65 billion to $666.93 billion in the week ended June 26.
• Gold reserves decreased by $5.39 billion to stand at $102.53 billion during the week.
• Foreign currency assets (FCAs), the largest component of the reserves, declined by $150 million to $5541.07 billion.

title: Food inflation projected to average 6% in FY27 amid rainfall deficit concerns: CareEdge Ratings
content:
• CareEdge Ratings projects India's food inflation to average 6% and CPI inflation at 5% in FY27, citing a 41.5% monsoon rainfall deficit recorded between June 1 and June 29, 2026.
• Net Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) inflows reached USD 6.6 billion in April 2026, up significantly from USD 1.6 billion in April 2025, while remittances grew by 70% year-on-year in April 2026.
• The current account balance recorded a surplus of USD 4.7 billion in April 2026, but the overall Balance of Payments registered a deficit of USD 6.6 billion due to heavy capital outflows.

title: Auto and liquor brands remain leading advertisers for FIFA World Cup 2026: TAM Sports
content:
• Mahindra & Mahindra ranked as the top advertiser for FIFA World Cup 2026 in terms of ad volume on CTV, while United Spirits led the linear TV segment.
• Mahindra Thar, Mahindra XEV, and Mahindra BE collectively took up 24 per cent of the ad volume share in CTV and 30 per cent in linear TV for FIFA World Cup 2026.
• Linear TV performance's average ad volume per channel per match dropped by 14 per cent during FIFA World Cup 2026 compared to FIFA WC 2022, which was acquired by Viacom18 for $60 million.
